title: Duplicate top-level var assignments silently override the first
description: When a sync script's regex anchor matches the FIRST `var X = [...].join(...);` block but a later duplicate `X = [...].join(...);` (without `var`) exists in the same script tag, the duplicate wins at runtime, silently overriding the freshly synced data. Symptom: viewer Browse tab shows stale or empty data despite a successful sync run reporting N entries written.
detection:
After any sync that writes a top-level identifier, grep-assert exactly one assignment exists:
  ([regex]::Matches($content,'(?m)^(var\s+)?IDENT\s*=')).Count -eq 1
Flag any file where Count > 1.
remediation: 1) Add a post-sync invariant check that fails the build if duplicate assignments are detected. 2) Prefer a guarded assignment pattern (e.g. `if (typeof X === 'undefined') { var X = ... }`) so accidental duplicates are caught at parse time. 3) When fixing legacy duplicates, delete the stale block by line range, do NOT rely on the regex sync to replace it.
examples: # Good — single canonical assignment, post-sync grep asserts count==1
